spec_match.rationale: Names and exercises `NavigatorDeviceMemory`, `navigator.deviceMemory`, WorkerNavigator exposure, `Sec-CH-Device-Memory` through a server echo route, and Chrome 147's implementation-defined Android/desktop bucket limits.

interactive.rationale: Live-read, worker-probe, and HTTP-header buttons invoke real browser/server paths, while platform radios, RAM controls, presets, and fallback mode update the bucket chart and app decision in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now reads the live value and visualizes the old versus Chrome 147 bucket systems promised by the feature index blurb.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers unsupported API fallback, secure-context status, worker exposure absence, missing client-hint requests after opt-in, low/high implementation clamps, and power-of-two tie rounding.

references.rationale: The see-also section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6330376953921536 and the Device Memory API spec.

design_system.rationale: All local colors use shared CSS variables, button background overrides also set color, and the page is no longer a static code-card.

summary: The memory inspector now provides real controls, live API probes, a server-backed `Sec-CH-Device-Memory` echo, bucket-limit simulation, and honest fallback behavior when the API or request header is absent.
